Patch Set 3:

afaiu, this is called on every RPC essentially? if so, then taking a sleepable semaphore isn't that good?

Patch Set 3:

This should only be called when identity isn't cached, and it seems to me that rw semaphore is the reasonable measure to do such kind of job. What's your major concern?

parents.subject: LU-8371 llite: optimize atomic_open of negative dentry.

commit.subject: LU-6447 mdt: mdt_identity_upcall to not block with rwlock held
commit.message:
LU-6447 mdt: mdt_identity_upcall to not block with rwlock held

mdt_identity_upcall is currently calling call_usermodehelper
with an rwlock held, which is a no-no since it allocates memory
and schedules. Just replace the rwlock with a rw_semaphore.

Change-Id: I7b063a4db47313fbae6241da7bcec2c397b8e8c4
Signed-off-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14432
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
